response.justification: While this candidate is a highly skilled senior developer with excellent communication abilities and strong programming fundamentals, they lack the core mandatory skills for this role. Most critically, they have only ~6 months of C++ experience (ending 2015) with no Windows platform background, no SQL Server/Oracle expertise, and zero capital markets or fixed-income domain experience. Their recent 10-year trajectory focuses on JavaScript/TypeScript and web systems, making them fundamentally misaligned with a Windows-based C++ capital markets role.
strengths: Senior-level seniority with 18+ years professional development experience and demonstrated leadership across multiple companies and roles
Excellent communication and teaching ability evidenced by workshops, talks, open-source contributions, and course development
Strong general programming fundamentals with proven ability to learn multiple languages and adapt across diverse technical domains
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (only ~6 months at National Instruments in 2015) with no hands-on C++ work in the last 10 years, replaced by JavaScript/TypeScript focus
No capital markets, fixed-income, derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge—the alternate mandatory requirement for domain expertise is also not met
No demonstrated Windows platform expertise, SQL Server/Oracle database experience, or STL proficiency; background is primarily Linux, web stack, and modern cloud technologies

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap and time away from language
Absence of capital markets or financial services domain experience
Recent technology stack misalignment with Windows/enterprise database requirements
